text: The surgeon visually inspects the base of the appendix at its junction with the cecum, noting its normal appearance. The appendectomy proceeds with careful manipulation to avoid direct contact with the mucocille, minimizing the risk of rupture.
The surgeon visually inspects the base of the appendix at its junction with the cecum, noting its normal appearance before proceeding with the appendectomy.
The surgeon carefully manipulates the mesentery with their left hand, avoiding direct contact with the mucocille of the appendix to prevent rupture.

The surgeon uses their left hand to grasp the mesentery for manipulation, carefully retracting it back to the base of the cecum. The mesentery is dissected from the cecum, removing slightly more tissue than typical for a standard appendectomy.
The surgeon carefully dissects the mesentery back to the base of the cecum, removing additional mesentery tissue from the cecum compared to a typical appendectomy.
The surgeon carefully positions away from the ileocecal valve and performs a partial cecectomy, removing the appendix.
The surgeon performs a partial cecectomy, carefully avoiding the ileocecal valve, while removing the appendix.
The surgeon places the appendix into a specimen bag for removal, ensuring to upsize the trocar to prevent rupture during extraction.
The surgeon places the appendix into a specimen bag for removal, ensuring the trocar is upsized to prevent inadvertent rupture during extraction.
